name: Video Raster
one_liner: A video frame being drawn line-by-line: glowing horizontal scanlines sweep down resolving a raster of pixels, then dissolve into a stream of discrete IP packets and reassemble -- SDI-to-IP made literal, with chroma separation.
visual_description: A near-black panel where a bright scanline sweeps top-to-bottom, drawing the image line by line like a CRT raster. Above the active line the picture is resolved (a shimmering field of fine cyan/green pixels with a subtle moving pattern); below it the panel is dark, waiting to be painted, with a faint phosphor afterglow trailing the sweep. The active scanline is white-hot and blooms. Then, on a steady PTP beat, the left portion of each resolved row PACKETIZES: the solid video bar breaks into a marching train of discrete glowing packet-blocks (quantized cells with gaps) that drift right -- the act of putting essence into RTP packets -- and on the right they reassemble back into a clean line. At the moving boundary where the picture is freshly drawn, the three color channels tear apart by a few pixels (RGB chromatic separation) then re-register into clean white. A gold field-rate counter feel, faint scanline/CRT overlay, and film grain finish the broadcast look. It reads instantly as 'the video in video-over-IP,' caught mid-transmission between a solid frame and a packet stream.
technique: Single self-contained GLSL TOP generator (no input). The shader synthesizes a procedural 'image' (a cheap moving pattern of fine cells in the signal palette), then applies a raster reveal: a sweeping scanline whose position advances with time gates which rows are 'drawn,' with a phosphor afterglow below the active line. A horizontal packetization function quantizes each drawn row into discrete packet-blocks (cell + gap) that drift and reassemble, mixed in by a Packetize amount on the PTP beat. RGB chromatic separation is applied at the active scanline boundary. Scanline/CRT overlay + grain + bloom finish. Cheap per-pixel math, no feedback, no input -- drops in anywhere.

network_plan.name: glsl_raster
network_plan.role: The whole piece. Generator (no input): procedural cell image, raster reveal scanline + phosphor afterglow, horizontal packetization, RGB chroma separation at the active line, PTP beat. Generator MUST use uTDOutputInfo.res; NEVER uTD2DInfos on a no-input generator. outputresolution custom 1280x720, rgba16float. Three vec4 uniforms via the vec SEQUENCE (declared manually). Place nodeX 400, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom
network_plan.role: Bloom + finish. Input0 = glsl_raster. Bright-pass + bounded blur added back for the scanline/packet glow; a heavier scanline/CRT overlay; film grain. outputresolution custom 1280x720, rgba16float. Place nodeX 800, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: out1
network_plan.role: Manifest output_op / capture target, named out1. Viewer flagged. Place nodeX 1200, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: glsl_raster_pixel
network_plan.role: Docked pixel shader for glsl_raster. See glsl[0]. extension frag.

network_plan.name: glsl_raster_compute
network_plan.role: Docked empty compute companion for glsl_raster.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom_pixel
network_plan.role: Docked pixel shader for glsl_bloom. See glsl[1]. extension frag.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom_compute
network_plan.role: Docked empty compute companion for glsl_bloom.

network_plan.name: annotate_raster
network_plan.role: Teaching annotation over the raster generator (glsl_raster). Mode annotate, Opacity 0.3.

params.Bodytext: One generator GLSL TOP. A scanline sweeps top-to-bottom drawing the image line by line (CRT raster) with a phosphor afterglow below the active line. On the PTP beat each drawn row PACKETIZES -- the solid video bar quantizes into discrete glowing packet-blocks that drift right and reassemble (essence into RTP packets). RGB chroma tears at the active line then re-registers. This is the 'video' in video-over-IP, made literal.

network_plan.name: annotate_finish
network_plan.role: Teaching annotation over bloom + out (glsl_bloom, out1). Mode annotate.

params.Bodytext: Bloom blows the white-hot scanline and packet-blocks into halos; a CRT scanline overlay + film grain sell the broadcast look. Terminates in out1.

exposed_params.name: Scanrate
exposed_params.label: Scan Rate
exposed_params.drives: uRaster.x + the time term uRaster.w -- how fast the raster scanline sweeps top-to-bottom (frame draw rate). 0 freezes the sweep (and drops that time-dependence); higher = a fast refresh.

exposed_params.name: Linecount
exposed_params.label: Line Count
exposed_params.drives: uRaster.y (row quantization) and uBloom.w (CRT scanline frequency) -- number of horizontal raster lines. Low = chunky retro scanlines; high = a fine modern raster.

exposed_params.name: Afterglow
exposed_params.label: Phosphor Afterglow
exposed_params.drives: uRaster.z -- length of the phosphor trail below the active scanline. 0 = a hard edge; higher = a long glowing CRT persistence tail.

exposed_params.name: Packetize
exposed_params.label: Packetize
exposed_params.drives: uPacket.x -- how strongly the left portion of each drawn row breaks into discrete packet-blocks on the PTP beat (SDI->IP dissolve). 0 = a solid frame; 1 = full packetization.

exposed_params.name: Packetsize
exposed_params.label: Packet Size
exposed_params.drives: uPacket.y -- width of each packet-block (in screen UV). Small = many fine packets; large = chunky blocks. The granularity of the IP packet stream.

exposed_params.name: Driftspeed
exposed_params.label: Packet Drift
exposed_params.drives: uPacket.z -- how fast the packet-blocks drift rightward (toward reassembly). The transport motion of the packets across the row.

exposed_params.name: Chromasplit
exposed_params.label: Chroma Split
exposed_params.drives: uPacket.w -- strength of the RGB chromatic separation at the active scanline boundary. 0 = clean; higher = a pronounced broadcast color tear that re-registers away from the line.

exposed_params.name: Detail
exposed_params.label: Image Detail
exposed_params.drives: uLook.x -- fineness of the procedural source image cells. Low = broad shapes; high = a dense fine-pixel field.

exposed_params.name: Palette
exposed_params.label: Palette Hue
exposed_params.drives: uLook.z -- rotates the cyclic signal palette of the source image through the hue ring. 0 = cyan/green signal look; sweeping shifts the chroma.

exposed_params.name: Ptprate
exposed_params.label: PTP Beat Rate
exposed_params.drives: uLook.y -- frequency of the PTP sync pulse that triggers each packetization burst. The transmission heartbeat.

exposed_params.name: Scanlines
exposed_params.label: CRT Scanlines
exposed_params.drives: uBloom.z -- strength of the CRT scanline darkening overlay in the finish pass. 0 = clean digital; higher = a heavier broadcast-CRT texture.

exposed_params.name: Bloom
exposed_params.label: Bloom
exposed_params.drives: uBloom.x -- intensity of the bloom over the scanline and packet-blocks. The booth-scale glow knob.

palette.ratio: ~85% dark ground + undrawn rows, ~15% saturated image signal + white-hot scanline; cyan/green lead, RGB-split is an accent event at the active line, not constant.

conference_fit: The literal 'video' motif -- a frame drawn line by line as a CRT raster, caught mid-transmission dissolving into IP packets and reassembling. It directly stages SDI->IP packetization (essence into RTP packets), the core of why ST2110 exists, plus the broadcast-authentic CRT scanline + RGB chroma-tear language every video engineer knows. Dark, premium, on-brand restraint with color appearing only as the signal. Self-contained and cheap; reads as a recognizable 'this is the video being transmitted' beat that complements the abstract packet/fiber pieces.

prompt.one_liner: A self-contained video-raster generator: a scanline sweeps down drawing the image line by line with phosphor afterglow, then on a PTP beat each row packetizes into drifting glowing blocks and reassembles, with RGB chroma separation at the active line and a CRT bloom finish.
prompt.evolve: Raise Scan Rate for a faster frame draw; lower Line Count for chunky retro scanlines; raise Afterglow for long CRT persistence. Push Packetize + Packet Drift for a stronger SDI->IP dissolve; shrink Packet Size for finer packets. Raise Chroma Split + CRT Scanlines for a heavier broadcast tear. Sweep Palette Hue for a different signal color. Feed out1 into the kaleidoscope, or composite under the packet-fabric swarm so packets exit the raster INTO the fabric. To extend: replace sourceImage() with a sampled input (a Movie File In into a real input) for an actual SDI->IP demo of any footage.

build_notes:
GENERATOR CORRECTNESS: glsl_raster has NO input -- MUST use uTDOutputInfo.res (res=(1/w,1/h,w,h)); uTD2DInfos on a no-input generator is a compile error. outputresolution MUST be 'custom' + resolutionw/h or it renders 1x1. glsl_bloom HAS an input so sTD2DInputs[0] + uTDOutputInfo.res are valid. NOTE: glsl_raster has NO loops at all (it is pure per-pixel math, sourceImage called a few times); glsl_bloom has the only loops, bounded by constant R=6.

UNIFORMS: glslTOP declares uniforms MANUALLY. vec parameter SEQUENCE (op.seq.vec.numBlocks=N, vecNname, vecNtype='vec4', vecNvaluex..w expressions), NOT op.par.vec.

COOK DEMAND: time-dependent via absTime in uRaster.w, uLook.w. Cooks every frame only when out1 demanded; add a temp Execute DAT OUTSIDE the COMP in the sandbox; verify with two captures separated by a real sleep. Scan Rate=0 freezes the sweep but uLook.w still references absTime (PTP beat + image motion), so the TOP keeps cooking -- correct.

PERFORMANCE: cheap -- a handful of sourceImage() evals + the bloom tap loop, no feedback, no big loops. Keep TOPs <=1280x720 rgba16float. Gate before/after per performance.md; a concurrent capture_top/numpyArray dips the fps reading, so take a clean reading.

DOCKED DATS: glsl_raster + glsl_bloom each dock pixel+compute+info; HUG all six under their hosts in the SAME execute_python that creates each op, then get_network_layout. LF/space-indented shader source.

LOOK CHECK: this piece has several blending modes (raster reveal, afterglow, packetize, chroma) -- it MUST be judged by an actual capture (load visual-aesthetics). Verify the scanline reads clearly, the packetization is legible as discrete blocks (not mush), and the chroma tear re-registers. Tune Packet Size / Packetize defaults against a real frame before persisting.

TDN ROUND-TRIP: all 12 params single-component (Int/Float) -> default->value seeding works on re-import (verified v6.0.26). Re-import into a throwaway COMP, confirm a couple .vals + out1 renders, destroy it. operator_count = all ops incl docked DATs (annotations not counted): ~9.
